title: use-after-free: async-file callback fires after the FileStreamer is destroyed on request cancel
category: use-after-free (bare `this` captured into an async callback guaranteed to fire even after cancel)
crash_site: Envoy ASAN heap-use-after-free in the file_server HTTP filter when a client cancels a request that is serving a file: an async filesystem callback runs after the request's FileStreamer was destroyed.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: the AsyncFileManager stat()/read() callback bodies and FileStreamer::readBodyChunk where the deref crashes — correct in themselves; the defect is the bare [this] capture in the methods that issue the async ops (begin/startDir/readBodyChunk).
ground_truth.fix_summary: FileStreamer captured a bare [this] into the callbacks returned by AsyncFileManager::stat()/read(). The async-file manager guarantees the callback fires even if cancel() was called, so when the FileStreamer is destroyed (request cancelled) before the callback runs, the [this] dangles and the callback dereferences freed memory. Fix: guard the callbacks against running after destruction (cancel/weak-handle the captured this).

admission.note: Authored from PR #45153 commit-message+diff. crash != cause: the async-file callback fires after request cancel and derefs a destroyed FileStreamer; cause is the bare [this] capture in the methods issuing the async ops (begin/startDir/readBodyChunk). Decoy = the callback body / readBodyChunk crash site. Pure C++ lifetime across an async boundary. Run baseline first.

provenance: https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/pull/45153 — file_server: fix UAF on async-file callback after request cancel; fix 96294652f7f2, base 671bb592d454.
